name: codebase-audit
description: Parallel codebase analysis: architecture, security, quality, and synthesis

tasks.subject: Inventory the codebase
tasks.description: Survey /home/shared/inputs/repo/. Map the directory structure, identify languages, frameworks, entry points, and dependencies. Write findings to /home/shared/inventory.md and register as artifact.

tasks.subject: Analyze system architecture
tasks.description: Based on /home/shared/inventory.md, document the high-level architecture: components, data flow, external dependencies, and coupling. Write to /home/shared/analysis/architecture.md

tasks.subject: Security audit
tasks.description: Scan /home/shared/inputs/repo/ for vulnerabilities: hardcoded secrets, injection risks, auth issues, OWASP top 10. Write findings to /home/shared/analysis/security.md

tasks.subject: Code quality analysis
tasks.description: Review /home/shared/inputs/repo/ for code smells, dead code, complexity, naming conventions, and error handling patterns. Write to /home/shared/analysis/code-quality.md

tasks.subject: Test coverage and quality assessment
tasks.description: Analyze test files in /home/shared/inputs/repo/. Assess coverage, identify untested paths, evaluate test quality. Write to /home/shared/analysis/test-coverage.md

tasks.subject: Synthesize findings into final report
tasks.description: Read all analysis files in /home/shared/analysis/. Produce a comprehensive audit report with executive summary, detailed findings, risk ratings, and prioritized recommendations. Write to /home/shared/outputs/audit-report.md and register as artifact.

mail.subject: Swarm started: codebase-audit
mail.body:
You are leading a codebase audit. Start by inventorying the repo at /home/shared/inputs/repo/.

Your first task (inventory) is ready on the task board.

Run: task.sh ready --owner $(whoami)
